contributor_analysis.author: Andrea Laforgia
contributor_analysis.implication: Codebase is authored by single developer; refactoring decisions can be made swiftly without coordination overhead. Risk: single viewpoint may have blind spots; recommend peer review for architectural changes.

primary_reasons: New InputState variant additions (visual mode, command mode)
Bug fixes in key event handling
hottest_files.smell_connection: Divergent Change - high frequency indicates multiple responsibilities triggering edits

primary_reasons: New field additions (panels, cursor_shapes, search_pattern, selection_start, change_list, etc.)
New command handler registrations (30+ builtin commands)
hottest_files.smell_connection: Shotgun Surgery - each subsystem addition requires touching EditorState new(), snapshots, and register_builtin_commands

primary_reasons: New primitive registrations (filesystem, list/string ops, panel API)
Primitives for new editor features (browser, rainbow CSV, etc.)
Coupling to core submodule API changes
hottest_files.smell_connection: Feature Envy - bridge reacts to every core submodule change, indicating tight coupling

buffer_subsystem.change_risk: MEDIUM - buffer is relatively stable; mutations come via well-defined commands

cursor_subsystem.change_risk: HIGH - cursor state is tightly coupled to motion handlers (h/j/k/l/w/b/e/f/F/t/T/0/$

command_system.change_risk: CRITICAL - adding new command requires edits to 3+ files: command.rs (enum), editor_state.rs (handler), app.rs (dispatch), bridge.rs (Lisp interface)

responsibilities: InputState machine (Normal, Insert, Visual, Command modes)
input_handling_subsystem.change_risk: CRITICAL - all input state transitions centralized in handle_key_event (605 lines)

rendering_subsystem.change_risk: MEDIUM - renderer is well-isolated but app.rs reaches into internals

lisp_runtime_subsystem.change_risk: MEDIUM - runtime is stable; coupling to EditorState through bridge is the risk

editor_state_root.change_risk: CRITICAL - central aggregator touched by every subsystem; single point of coupling

smells: Divergent Change (modified for every new InputState variant, motion, or operator)
high_risk_subsystems.author: Andrea Laforgia
high_risk_subsystems.urgency: CRITICAL - core event loop, affects every user input

smells: Shotgun Surgery (adding subsystem requires touching new(), snapshots, register_builtin_commands)
high_risk_subsystems.author: Andrea Laforgia
high_risk_subsystems.urgency: CRITICAL - architectural foundation, all commands depend on it

smells: Feature Envy (5198 lines, 8 submodule accesses per primitive)
Message Chain (100+ direct field accesses into EditorState)
Tight Coupling (reacts to every core API change)
high_risk_subsystems.author: Andrea Laforgia
high_risk_subsystems.urgency: HIGH - extension boundary, must be maintainable for plugin authors

smells: Control Flow Complexity (each branch reaches into 5-8 core modules)
medium_risk_subsystems.author: Andrea Laforgia
medium_risk_subsystems.urgency: MEDIUM - users expect command extensibility (e.g., custom :q variant)

smells: Message Chain (app.rs must know renderer internals for redraw coordination)
Divergent Change (modified when adding panels, plugins, or themes)
medium_risk_subsystems.author: Andrea Laforgia
medium_risk_subsystems.urgency: MEDIUM - good isolation but coupling could be tighter

browser_feature.pattern: Feature required touching 6+ files across 3 crates; command/input dispatch added to app.rs; core API expanded

visual_mode_feature.pattern: Feature required EditorState field additions + InputState variant + visual-specific motion logic in app.rs

search_and_replace.pattern: Search state scattered across EditorState + app.rs; colon commands tightly coupled to TUI

macro_recording.pattern: Macro state in EditorState; dispatch logic in app.rs; isolated but fragile

refactoring_ownership_plan.decision_making: Can decide all refactoring directions unilaterally; recommend peer code review on:  (1) EditorFacade design (public API boundary), (2) InputStateMachine abstraction (complex logic), (3) Subsystem decomposition (architectural impact)
refactoring_ownership_plan.implementation_approach: Serial refactoring by component; feature freeze during Phase 1-2 (abstraction boundaries + TUI decomposition) recommended to avoid conflicts
refactoring_ownership_plan.testing_responsibility: All test updates owned by Andrea; no need to coordinate with other team members
refactoring_ownership_plan.documentation_responsibility: Update CLAUDE.md architectural sections after major refactorings; document new module responsibilities

git_history_recommendations.commit_granularity: Current commits are well-scoped (one feature per commit); continue this practice during refactorings
git_history_recommendations.branch_strategy: Create feature branches for each refactoring (e.g., feature/extract-input-machine, feature/editor-facade). Rebase onto main when ready.
git_history_recommendations.pull_request_strategy: Each refactoring rank 1-5 should be a separate PR for reviewability; ranks 6-11 can be grouped by phase
git_history_recommendations.history_preservation: No git rewriting recommended; linear history aids future archaeology of design decisions

future_contribution_guidance.new_commands: After refactoring #6 (data-driven command table), add new commands to crates/alfred-core/src/handlers/ module + BUILTIN_COMMANDS table entry
future_contribution_guidance.new_lisp_primitives: After refactoring #3 + #7 (EditorFacade + bridge extraction), add primitives to crates/alfred-lisp/src/bridge.rs using only EditorFacade methods
future_contribution_guidance.new_colon_commands: After refactoring #2 (ColonCommandDispatcher), add to crates/alfred-tui/src/colon_commands.rs registry
future_contribution_guidance.new_input_modes: After refactoring #1 (InputStateMachine), define new InputState variant in crates/alfred-tui/src/input_machine.rs + add process handler

removes_smells: Conditional Complexity (colon command match becomes registry lookup)
Divergent Change (app.rs no longer modified for new modes, commands, or motions)

refactoring_5_6_combined.estimated_bug_reduction: 20% (smaller code units = easier to reason about)

removes_smells: Feature Envy (bridge.rs accesses <= 1 module (EditorFacade) instead of 8)
Insider Trading (all core access mediated by facade)
refactoring_7.estimated_extensibility_gain: 60% easier for future plugin authors to understand Lisp primitive contracts
